qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded Memory Allocation in SSL Read Leading to Denial of Service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption, CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unbounded allocation in SSLSocket.read()/recv_into() from an attacker-influenced length and a denial-of-service impact. The advisory concerns verify_mode=CERT_REQUIRED missing WOLFSSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT, allowing mTLS client authentication bypass when no client certificate is provided.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The only qualifying finding is a separate SSLSocket.read()/recv_into() memory-allocation denial-of-service issue. The session's closer certificate-validation finding is non-qualifying and describes insecure default CERT_NONE behavior, not CERT_REQUIRED failing to enforce client-certificate presence due to the missing WOLFSSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT flag.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-vj87-jj27-4h9c describes wolfssl-py versions through 5.8.2 incorrectly handling verify_mode=CERT_REQUIRED because WOLFSSL_VERIFY_FAIL_IF_NO_PEER_CERT was not included, so peers without client certificates could be authenticated.
finding_003_unbounded_memory_allocation/vulnerability_report.json is the only final report with status=qualifying_vuln.

finding_003_unbounded_memory_allocation/vulnerability_report.json identifies wolfssl/__init__.py SSLSocket.read() and recv_into() allocations using byte[%d] with attacker-influenced lengths, causing memory exhaustion denial of service.

finding_002_missing_cert_validation/vulnerability_report.json is non_qualifying_vuln and describes default cert_reqs=CERT_NONE rather than the advisory's CERT_REQUIRED client-certificate enforcement bug.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 runtime classification is not applicable.
